text.content:
Before Every Session (Desktop)

The Foundry Electron app enforces browser CORS, blocking direct API calls. A local proxy must be running before you launch Foundry.

  1. Open a terminal in the module folder and run:
    ./proxy/start.sh (Mac/Linux) or proxy\start.bat (Windows)
    Or: npm run proxy
  2. Launch Foundry. The console should show:
    starforged-companion | Proxy reachable: Local proxy (http://127.0.0.1:3001)

On The Forge: no proxy needed — The Forge handles external API calls server-side automatically.

First-Time Setup

  1. Open Configure Settings → Starforged Companion
  2. Enter your Claude API Key (from console.anthropic.com)
  3. Enter your Art Generation API Key (OpenAI, optional — for portraits)
  4. Leave Claude Proxy URL as http://127.0.0.1:3001 unless using a custom port

Basic Workflow

Type your narration naturally in chat. The companion intercepts it, identifies the move, and shows a confirmation dialog. Accept to roll. The narrator responds after every move.

Example: "I throw myself at the controls and try to stabilise the ship before we hit the debris field."

To bypass the interpreter, start your message with \.

Chat Commands

Type these directly into the Foundry chat bar.

Core Commands

CommandEffect
type narration normallyIntercepted and routed through the move pipeline. Dice are rolled, a result card is posted, and the narrator responds.
\your message hereBypass the move interpreter entirely. Posts as normal chat — nothing is intercepted.
@scene your questionAsk the narrator to describe something about the current scene without triggering a move.
Example: @scene What do the hull markings look like up close?

Safety Commands

CommandEffect
/xX-Card. Immediately suppresses the current scene. Usable by any player at any time — no explanation required.

Recap Commands (GM only)

CommandEffect
/recapPost a campaign recap — the full story arc compressed to 3–5 paragraphs.
/recap campaignSame as /recap.
/recap sessionPost a recap of the current session's moves and outcomes.
/recap session 3Post a recap of a specific past session by number.

World Journal Commands (GM only)

CommandEffect
/journal faction "Name" attitude — summaryRecord faction intelligence. Attitude: hostile, neutral, allied, unknown.
/journal location "Name" type — descriptionRecord a location. Type: settlement, derelict, vault, planet, ship, other.
/journal lore "Title" confirmed — textRecord a lore discovery. Use "confirmed" or "rumour".
/journal threat "Name" severity — summaryRecord an active threat. Severity: looming, active, immediate, resolved.

Toolbar Buttons

Four buttons appear in the Foundry scene control toolbar (the icon strip on the left of the canvas, in the Token Controls group).

IconButtonDescription
🗂Progress TracksOpen the progress track panel. Create, mark, and complete vows, expeditions, combat tracks, connections, and legacy tracks. All tracks are stored in the "Starforged Progress Tracks" journal.
👥EntitiesOpen the entity panel. Browse all tracked Connections, Ships, Settlements, Factions, and Planets. Click any entity to see its detail view, portrait, and notes.
📖Character ChronicleOpen the character chronicle — a reverse-chronological record of significant story moments, revelations, and relationship developments. Players can add and edit entries freely.
🛡Companion SettingsOpen the settings panel (GM only). Configure Safety, Mischief dial, and Narrator settings.

Configure Settings → Starforged Companion

SettingDescriptionScope
Claude API KeyYour Anthropic API key. Stored in your browser only — never sent to Foundry's server. Get one at console.anthropic.com.Client (per browser)
Art Generation API KeyYour OpenAI API key for DALL-E 3 portrait generation. Optional.Client (per browser)
Art Generation BackendWhich art service to use. Currently: DALL-E 3 (OpenAI).World
Claude Proxy URLBase URL of the local Claude proxy. Default: http://127.0.0.1:3001. Change if you start the proxy with a custom port.World
Push-to-TalkEnable speech input via the browser's Web Speech API. Chromium browsers only. Adds a microphone button to the chat bar.Client
Speech Input LanguageBCP 47 language tag for speech recognition. Default: en-US.Client

Companion Settings Panel (Toolbar → 🛡)

Safety Tab

SettingDescription
LinesHard content limits. Never crossed under any circumstances. Set by GM, apply to all players.
VeilsSoft content limits. Approached carefully or faded to black. Set by GM.
Private LinesPersonal hard limits visible only to you and the GM. Set on your own client — never shared with other players.

Mischief Tab

SettingDescription
LawfulLiteral move interpretation. No misdirection. Asides are plain.
BalancedDefault. Occasional organic misreads that create interesting moments. Wry asides when interpretation is ambiguous.
ChaoticActively seeks unexpected move interpretations. Asides are openly smug about it.

Narrator Tab

SettingDescription
Narration ModelClaude model for narration. Sonnet 4.5 (recommended, richer prose) or Haiku 4.5 (faster, more economical).
PerspectiveAuto (second person "you" for solo, third person for multiplayer), or fixed to one.
ToneWry (default), Grim and grounded, Operatic, Noir, or Matter of fact.
LengthTarget sentence count per narration. Default: 3.
Custom InstructionsFree-text injected into the narrator system prompt. Use for campaign-specific style notes, character voice, setting details.

Scene Interrogation

SettingDescription
Scene Interrogation EnabledWhen on, messages starting with @scene ask the narrator to describe the scene without triggering a move.
Scene Response LengthTarget sentence count for scene responses. Default: 2.
Scene Context CardsNumber of recent narration cards included as scene context for each query. Default: 3.

The Narrator

After every accepted move, the narrator generates 2–4 sentences of atmospheric prose describing the mechanical consequence as story. It uses the full campaign context — world truths, active connections, safety configuration, character state — to stay grounded in what has been established.

What the narrator does

  • Narrates the mechanical consequence of the move outcome (Strong Hit, Weak Hit, Miss) as vivid prose
  • Stays within the established fiction — it illuminates what is already there, not invents new things
  • Respects all Lines and Veils unconditionally — safety is a hard ceiling
  • Maintains a wry tone by default — knowing, slightly sardonic, aware of consequence without wallowing
  • Adapts perspective automatically: second person ("you") for solo, third person (character names) for multiplayer

What the narrator does not do

  • Roll dice or make mechanical decisions — that is the move system
  • Introduce new plot elements not already established — use oracles for that
  • Override your interpretation of events — it narrates consequences, you narrate actions

Scene interrogation

Type @scene [your question] to ask the narrator to describe something without triggering a move. The narrator will describe what is already there, not invent new elements. This is the constraint by design — @scene is a camera, not a writer.

Cost

Estimated cost per 3-hour session (~20 moves) with prompt caching:

  • Haiku interpretation + Haiku narration: ~$0.02/session
  • Haiku interpretation + Sonnet narration: ~$0.06/session

Safety System

The safety system ensures all narration respects your content boundaries. Safety configuration is always the first section of every context packet sent to the narrator. It is never omitted, summarised, or overridden by any other setting.

X-Card

Type /x in chat at any time to activate the X-Card. The current scene is immediately suppressed. The narrator will not continue until the scene is redirected. No explanation required. Any player can use it at any time.

To clear the X-Card, the GM can run this in the Foundry console:
const s = game.settings.get('starforged-companion','campaignState'); s.xCardActive=false; await game.settings.set('starforged-companion','campaignState',s);

Lines and Veils

Lines are hard limits — content matching a Line will never appear regardless of any other setting. Set by GM in the Safety tab of the Companion Settings panel.

Veils are soft limits — the narrator will approach this content carefully, redirect, or fade to black.

Private Lines are personal hard limits visible only to you and the GM. Set on your own client — never visible to other players.

How safety is applied

Safety configuration is injected before any creative content in every narrator call. It is described to the narrator as a hard ceiling that overrides all other creative direction including the mischief dial setting.

Character Management

The module reads from and writes directly to the foundry-ironsworn Actor document. The character sheet is the single source of truth for all mechanical data.

Automatic meter updates

After every accepted move, the module automatically updates your character's meters:

  • Health — reduced by Endure Harm outcomes
  • Spirit — reduced by Endure Stress outcomes
  • Supply — reduced by Sacrifice Resources
  • Momentum — adjusted by move outcomes; momentum burn handled automatically
  • Debilities — set when mandatory suffer moves apply impacts
  • XP — awarded when legacy track boxes are filled

Note: meter updates require the GM to be the active client. If you are playing as a player character, the GM client processes persistence. See the Troubleshooting page if meters are not updating.

Character Chronicle

Open the Chronicle via the toolbar (📖 button). The Chronicle records significant story moments — revelations, relationship developments, vow milestones, scars, and legacy events.

  • The narrator automatically adds an entry after each narration
  • Players can add, edit, and annotate any entry freely
  • Pinned entries always appear in the narrator's context
  • Recent chronicle entries are included in every narration context packet

The Chronicle is stored in a Foundry journal named "Chronicle — [Character Name]". Do not delete this journal.

Starship

Ship damage (Battered, Cursed) is tracked on the starship Actor. The module finds your starship automatically — it must be a Foundry Actor of type "starship" owned by a player.

Progress Tracks

Open the Progress Track panel via the toolbar (🗂 button in Token Controls).

Track types

  • Vow — sworn iron vows. Rank determines progress per mark.
  • Expedition — journeys through the Forge.
  • Combat — active fights where progress is marked by gaining ground.
  • Connection — relationship tracks for NPCs. Linked to Connection entity records.
  • Legacy — Quests, Bonds, and Discoveries legacy tracks for XP.

Ranks and progress

RankTicks per mark
Troublesome3 ticks (≈3–4 marks to fill)
Dangerous2 ticks (≈5 marks)
Formidable1 tick (≈10 marks)
Extreme½ tick (≈20 marks)
Epic¼ tick (≈40 marks)

Each box = 4 ticks. 10 boxes = 40 ticks for a fully filled track.

Storage

All tracks are stored in a single journal named "Starforged Progress Tracks". Do not delete this journal.

Entities & Portraits

Open the Entity Panel via the toolbar (👥 button in Token Controls).

Entity types

  • Connections — NPCs with a relationship track and history log
  • Ships — vessels including your command ship
  • Settlements — outposts, stations, and communities
  • Factions — organisations, orders, and powers
  • Planets — worlds and moons

Portrait lifecycle

Each entity can have one AI-generated portrait (DALL-E 3). The portrait lifecycle is strict by design — it creates visual consistency for the campaign.

  1. No portrait — Generate button available once a source description exists in the narration
  2. Portrait (unlocked) — One regeneration permitted
  3. Portrait (locked) — Permanently locked after regeneration. No further changes.

Choose your regeneration carefully. The lock is permanent.

Storage

Each entity is stored in its own Foundry Journal Entry. Do not manually edit these journals — use the Entity Panel instead.

World Journal

The World Journal automatically documents things discovered during play. It lives in the Foundry Journals sidebar under a "Starforged Companion" folder.

Journal categories

  • Factions — Intelligence — what is known about organisations and powers, their goals and attitude
  • Locations — Known Sites — descriptions of visited or named places
  • Lore — Discoveries — revelations about the setting, precursors, AI history, and mysteries
  • Threats — Active Dangers — tracked threats with severity status (looming → active → immediate → resolved)
  • Session Log — automatically written at session end

How entries are created

Automatic: After every narration, the module analyses the text and records any world-significant events (faction encounters, location descriptions, lore revelations, new threats).

Manual: Use /journal commands to record things directly (see Chat Commands page). This is also how to correct auto-detected entries or add things the module missed.

Annotations

Any player can annotate any world journal entry. Annotations are marked with your name and date. The module's auto-generated content is fully editable — this is your world record.

Troubleshooting

"Proxy not running" warning on load

The Claude API proxy is not running. Start it before launching Foundry:
./proxy/start.sh (Mac/Linux) or proxy\start.bat (Windows)

Move interpretation failed — API error

Check your Claude API Key in Configure Settings. It should start with sk-ant-. Make sure there is no trailing whitespace. The key is stored per-browser — if you switch browsers you will need to re-enter it.

Narration unavailable after move card

The narrator API call failed. Check the browser console (F12) for the specific error. Common causes: proxy not running, wrong API key, incorrect model name in Narrator settings.

Character sheet not updating after moves

Meter changes require the GM client to be active — the module gates world-scoped writes to the GM. If you are playing as a player character, make sure the GM account is connected. If you are the GM and meters still do not update, check the console for errors from actorBridge.js or persistResolution.js.

No move card after typing narration

The message may have been filtered. Check: you are not logged in as the GM (GM messages are excluded), the message does not start with \, /, or @, and the message type is not OOC or a roll.

Safety settings not applying

Safety settings sync to campaign state on the ready hook and on every write. If changes do not appear to apply, reload the world (F5).

X-Card will not clear

Run this in the Foundry console (F12) as the GM:
const s = game.settings.get('starforged-companion','campaignState'); s.xCardActive=false; await game.settings.set('starforged-companion','campaignState',s);

Progress tracks journal is missing

The "Starforged Progress Tracks" journal was deleted. The module will recreate it automatically on the next track operation. Existing track data cannot be recovered if the journal was deleted.

Portrait generation failed

Check your Art Generation API Key (OpenAI). Portrait generation requires an active OpenAI account with credits. DALL-E 3 costs approximately $0.04 per portrait at standard quality.

Chronicle not recording

The Chronicle journal is created automatically on first use. If entries are not appearing, check that the narrator is running successfully (narration card should appear after moves) and that chronicleAutoEntry is enabled in Companion Settings.

Changelog

v0.5.0

  • Added: Scene interrogation — type @scene your question to ask the narrator about the current scene without triggering a move
  • Added: Scene card appears in chat with question visible and narrator response below
  • Added: Scene queries are suppressed when the X-Card is active
  • Added: Three new settings: Scene Interrogation Enabled, Scene Response Length, Scene Context Cards

v0.4.0

  • Added: Session ID management — each world load generates or restores a session ID; resumes existing session if last session was less than 4 hours ago
  • Added: Session number increments automatically on each new session
  • Added: Narrator cards now carry session ID, session number, move ID, outcome, and narration text as flags — enables upcoming recap and scene interrogation features
  • Added: About tab in Companion Settings shows current session number, session ID, and session start time

v0.3.0

  • Added: Character management — move resolutions now automatically update health, spirit, momentum, debilities, and XP on the foundry-ironsworn character sheet
  • Added: Character Chronicle — reverse-chronological story record with player annotations; accessible via the 📖 toolbar button
  • Added: Starship damage tracking — Battered and Cursed impacts applied to the starship Actor automatically
  • Added: Momentum burn handled automatically when action die matches current momentum

v0.2.0

  • Added: Direct Claude narration replacing Loremaster — works from any player account, no GM dependency
  • Added: Narrator tab in Companion Settings — configure model (Haiku/Sonnet), perspective, tone, length, and custom instructions
  • Added: Auto perspective — second person for solo campaigns, third person for multiplayer
  • Added: Default tone: wry (knowing, slightly sardonic)
  • Added: Prompt caching for narration system prompt — significantly reduces per-session cost
  • Fixed: ChatMessage type "other" removed (not valid in Foundry v13)
  • Fixed: message.author deprecation warning in v13
  • Fixed: Meter persistence now correctly gated to GM account

v0.1.x — Deployment hardening

  • Fixed: CORS — local Node.js proxy added; The Forge server-side proxy supported
  • Fixed: Foundry v13 compatibility (ApplicationV2, DOM API, string literal message types)
  • Fixed: World truths, progress tracks, and X-Card suppression now working correctly
  • Fixed: Safety configuration now correctly reaches the narrator context packet
  • Fixed: Mischief dial "lawful" value now recognised correctly
  • Fixed: CI release — module.json version and URLs updated before zip build

v0.1.0 — Initial release

  • Move interpretation via Claude Haiku with prompt caching
  • Move confirmation dialog, dice resolution, mischief dial
  • Safety system (Lines, Veils, Private Lines, X-Card)
  • Progress tracks, entity panel, portrait generation (DALL-E 3)
  • World Truths generator, oracle tables, push-to-talk speech input
  • Foundry v13 compatibility
